pattern: Product-equivalence is a running invariant -- extending a window by one element only ever needs its running product, gcd and lcm updated, so every window can be checked incrementally instead of recomputed from scratch.
hints: prod == lcm * gcd is just a property you can check after multiplying in one more element; there's no need to recompute a window's product, gcd, or lcm from its raw elements each time.
For every starting index, extend the window rightward while updating a running product, a running gcd, and a running lcm one element at a time, and check the equality after each extension.

Track the best window length seen the moment the running product equals the running lcm times the running gcd; because values are small (at most 10) the running numbers stay manageable even though the raw product can grow large.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, best window skips one element
ones surrounding two disjoint short runs
minimum length, single pair already equivalent
all ones, entire array trivially equivalent
a single large value breaks an otherwise-equivalent run

stress.description: random small values near the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
import math


class Solution:
    def maxLength(self, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        best = 0
        for i in range(n):
            prod = 1
            g = 0
            l = 1
            for j in range(i, n):
                v = nums[j]
                prod *= v
                g = math.gcd(g, v)
                l = l * v // math.gcd(l, v)
                if prod == l * g:
                    best = max(best, j - i + 1)
        return best
